entries.title: Play Points members and comic book fans can score exclusive rewards with Google Play
entries.summary: Overview of The Auction by Google Play
entries.content:
Google Play is returning to Comic-Con® International with even more ways for Google Play Points members to get rewarded — both on the ground and online. Starting today, you can visit our immersive auction house live in San Diego to score physical collectibles from fan favorites like MARVEL Strike Force and Crunchyroll, plus unlock exclusive perks and rare rewards from anywhere with our digital rewards case and Play Points Use tab. Here’s what to look for.

Score exclusive rewards on Google Play

There’s something for every Google Play Points member, and the more points you have, the more ways you can redeem them. Here’s how.

  • Digital rewards case: Starting today, Gold+ members can unlock an exclusive digital rewards case on Google Play for a special chance to score points boosters and surprise collectibles 1. Just look for it on the You tab or the Perks tab of Play Points home.
  • Use tab: For instant rewards, head to the Use tab, where all Play Points members can redeem points for unique, high-demand gear, including the sold-out Gen 2 Backbone One | DEATH STRANDING 2 Limited Edition controller.

Visit The Auction by Google Play

For those joining us on the ground in San Diego, get ready for The Auction by Google Play. From July 23 to 25, step into an immersive space where tokens earned on-site are your currency. Kick off the fun at our giant gacha. Then move through the space knowing everyone walks away with rewards, whether you’re redeeming tokens for a custom tote bag or uniquely crafted keepsakes at the keychain charm bar or trading them at the rewards counter.

Just make sure to save some tokens for the grand finale on July 25 at 5 p.m. PT, featuring a live auction hosted by Quenlin Blackwell, where you can bid live on premium, high-value rewards, including an Emerald Gemagotchi Necklace with white diamond buttons on a 14K gold ball chain, a Herman Miller Vantum Gaming Chair, and more.

If you’re a Play Points member, you get perks right at the door with priority fast-track entry to both the general space and the live auction, along with a stash of special bonus tokens at check-in scaled to your membership tier. After checking in, members will unlock exclusive rewards.

Not a member? Join the Google Play Points program to get more of what you want for doing what you love.

entries.title: Apply now for the Google for Startups Gemini Startup Forum.
entries.summary: Learn more about Google for Startups Gemini Startup Forum and apply by August 28.
entries.content:
Applications are open for our two-day, Bay Area Forum supporting innovative startups that are using AI. Created in partnership with Google DeepMind and Google Cloud, the Gemini Startup Forum connects the founders of seed to Series A startups from around the world directly with the engineers building Google’s latest AI models.

Selected founders will:

  • Explore how Google AI can be used and strategically integrated
  • Learn from Google engineers how to scale a product with Gemini
  • Get practical tips on key business topics, such as organizational design and funding practices
  • Debate, co-create and engage with a global peer network

Spots are limited; apply to the Google for Startups Gemini Startup Forum by August 28, 2026.

The Gemini Startup Forum is part of the Google for Startups Gemini Kit, which is packed with APIs, tools, training and technical resources to help startups scale with AI.

entries.title: Understanding the AI economy
entries.summary: "AI & Economy Atlas" with Google logo, all over a green grid background
entries.content:
There is broad agreement that AI’s potential to transform the global economy and the way we work is significant. However, the outcomes – what this means for work, for people’s lives, and the economy writ large – are not automatic nor guaranteed. A lot has to happen. To get there, we as a society must work together to positively shape how AI impacts our lives, jobs, and economy. In order for this shared work to be effective, it is critical to have a rich understanding of how AI is being adopted and used in the economy. Society needs empirical insights and evidence-based research to inform decisions, initiatives, and actions.

To help, Google is launching the first iteration of the AI & Economy ATLAS (Activity, Task, Landscape, and Adoption Study), an ongoing, large-scale, de-identified study of how people are using Google’s AI products and tools. ATLAS’s first dataset (v1.0) is built from 15 million aggregated and de-identified human-AI interactions across the Gemini App, AI Mode, and the Gemini API, which together are used by more than 1 billion people monthly. ATLAS v1.0 insights span more than 150 countries, 140 languages, 800 occupations, and 4,000 tasks; ATLAS is the most comprehensive look to date at how real people are using AI at scale.

ATLAS sheds light on how people are using Google’s AI tools for various tasks at work and in their day-to-day lives. The ATLAS v1.0 report provides an early view of a quickly moving landscape: AI’s capabilities are advancing, its use is evolving, and tools for observing its impact on the economy are still a work-in-progress.

What are we learning from ATLAS v1.0?

Here a few of the most interesting observations so far:

  • AI use at work is broad but shallow: Workplace adoption spans all industry sectors and also 68% of all occupations that collectively represent 90% of total U.S. employment. However within jobs, people are using AI selectively: in a typical job AI is used for only ~21% of tasks.
  • At work, most AI use is focused on collaboration and assistance with tasks, and so far task automation is uncommon: ATLAS data shows the vast majority of AI interactions at work focus on collaborative uses such as ideation, strategy, information retrieval, and learning. Tasks like creative design and hypothesis testing (categorized in ATLAS as “non-routine cognitive”) show up in AI work interactions at a much higher rate than in the economy as a whole (65% vs 35%). Less than 10% of those interactions fully automate tasks.
  • AI use is not limited to white collar workers, it’s also assisting workers in predominantly physical and manual occupations with adjacent tasks: AI use for work is not limited to jobs traditionally seen as knowledge work. While not as prevalent, workers in manual and technical trades (e.g., auto technicians, industrial mechanics) are using conversational AI as a live collaborator for real-time diagnostics, troubleshooting, and on-the-fly learning. When workers in these areas use our AI tools, they’re 2x more likely to use multimodal AI (i.e. using AI to create images or video). For example, automotive technicians and industrial mechanics use AI to interpret complex test results, debug electrical wiring, and inspect machinery for wear.
  • AI is delivering value at home that may be missed in standard economic metrics, particularly around high-friction administrative tasks: Over 86% of interactions with AI tools in ATLAS occur outside of work. People are using AI in new and interesting ways not captured in standard economic metrics including productive household activities (e.g. researching purchases, help with using appliances, and tools) and high-friction administrative tasks (e.g. navigating government services like taxes, licensing, and fines).
  • Global AI adoption is tracking GDP per capita, with notable exceptions: AI usage has diffused globally. ATLAS data shows AI usage in over 150 countries and territories that represent 99% of the world's population. We also see this in the diversity of languages used in ATLAS. English represents only about a third of global AI conversations, and users do not systematically abandon their native languages for complex tasks. Looking more deeply, on a per-capita basis AI usage closely mirrors a country’s relative level of wealth, raising concerns about a persisting digital divide. However this isn’t a universal rule: some middle-income countries in South America and the Middle East are adopting AI at rates comparable to higher-income countries.

Here are some additional findings:

How ATLAS has been developed

ATLAS insights are powered by Google DeepMind’s Observation Clustering and Taxonomy Organisation (OCTO), a tool for transforming massive unstructured text data, like LLM conversations, and distilling them into organized entities. ATLAS has been developed with the highest level of privacy protections. In addition to scrubbing personally identifiable information (PII), we added several additional layers of protection that automatically remove any possible references to sensitive information, remove all linkages between de-identified ATLAS data and underlying user logs, summarize the text data, and aggregate summaries into groups representing multiple users.

What’s next?

This is just the beginning — the ATLAS v1.0 represents the start of a long-term project. AI’s capabilities continue to expand, people are continuing to find new and interesting ways to use it, and research methodologies to understand AI continue to evolve. There are many more questions around AI and the economy where more work will be needed — work that Google’s AI & Economy Research Program is undertaking in collaboration with academic and other researchers.

And there is a much wider range of economically-relevant AI usage not reflected in ATLAS. These include AI-enabled products with billions of users and interactions like Google Workspace, Google Translate, and AI Overviews; enterprise platforms, such as Gemini for Google Cloud and Gemini Enterprise; and frontier capabilities in several key areas, such as agentic coding and world models.

As we continue to build upon ATLAS and expand its scope and capabilities to generate new insights, we aim to provide a sharper understanding of the AI-driven transformation of the economy. We hope it is helpful to researchers, policymakers, businesses, workers, and other participants in the economy as we work together to shape the ways AI can positively support people in their lives.

We’d like to acknowledge Dame Diane Coyle (Cambridge) and Dr. David Autor (MIT) for their contributions to the ATLAS v1.0 report, as well as the entire ATLAS team 1 .

entries.title: Introducing selfie for sign-in: a new, easy way to access your Google Account
entries.summary: Sizzle video overviewing using selfie video to verify identity
entries.content:
Your Google Account holds a lot of valuable information, from cherished photos to important emails and documents. We always want to make sure that information is private, secure, and readily available to you, which is why we recommend setting up multiple sign-in methods. Today, we’re adding a new way to sign in: selfie video.

Selfie video is a new way to get into your account, giving you more options if you’re ever locked out or don’t have access to your usual phone or computer.

How to set up and use selfie video

Setting up a selfie video is simple: You just look at your device's camera and complete a few short, guided head movements to capture multiple angles.

If you’re having trouble signing in later on, you just take another selfie to get back into your account. Selfie video compares the new video to the one you set up to confirm it is really you and help you get back into your account.

Built with privacy and security in mind

Your selfie video is yours and you’re in control. It is recorded and securely stored with your consent, and you can delete it at any time in your Google Account. It’s used only for helping you sign in, unless you opt to share it for additional purposes. Your selfie video is encrypted at rest, meaning it's securely stored even when it's not being used.

When you use a selfie to sign in, we use multiple layers of security to help prevent impersonation attempts like fake photos and videos (i.e., deep fakes). For example, we match your video against your saved selfie and require you to perform simple movements to prove it's a live video. We also use our standard security practices to detect and help prevent suspicious sign-in attempts.

This update builds on our ongoing work to provide secure and flexible sign-in options, like passkeys and recovery contacts. You can check if your account is eligible and set up your selfie video today at g.co/signin-selfie.

entries.title: Q2 2026 earnings call: Remarks from our CEO
entries.summary: Read an edited transcript of Sundar Pichai’s remarks from the Q2 2026 Alphabet earnings call.
entries.content:
We have exciting momentum. Alphabet revenue grew 24% year-over-year. Our AI investments are redefining what’s possible across every part of our business.

Our momentum starts with Search, where people are adopting one seamless search experience across AI Overviews and AI Mode. We saw 17% revenue growth in Search and Other. And YouTube Ads grew 13%.

Cloud revenue grew 82%, powered by strong demand for AI infrastructure and AI solutions. And Cloud backlog grew to $514 billion. It’s great to see the wide adoption of Gemini Enterprise, with nearly 90% of the Fortune 100 using it.

Today, I’ll expand on our AI progress, our global product footprint led by Search and YouTube, Google Cloud’s leadership, and our long-term Bets.

AI progress

Yesterday we announced new models — Gemini 3.6 Flash and Gemini 3.5 Flash-Lite — which are cost effective and highly efficient. We’re seeing tons of demand for our workhorse Gemini Flash series, because it hits the sweet spot of performance and cost.

We also launched Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber, which I’m really excited about. Paired with our CodeMender agent, it finds and fixes vulnerabilities and delivers performance at the frontier, comparable to far bigger cyber models.

Gemini 3.5 Pro is currently in testing. And our team is already building the next generation of models. We have started our most ambitious pre-training run yet, for Gemini 4, and are excited by the progress we are seeing at the frontier.

Demand for our models is translating to strong token usage across developers and enterprise customers, and we continue to be supply constrained — a sign of momentum and rapid adoption.

  • More than 9 million developers are building each month with our models across our APIs and key developer products.
  • Our model APIs are now processing approximately 22 billion tokens per minute. That’s up from 16 billion just a quarter ago.

Additionally this quarter, we launched Omni. It allows users to create anything from any input, starting with video. Since launching at I/O in May, there’s been a 40% increase in daily active users creating videos on the Gemini app.

Our Gemma family of open models small enough to run on local devices are hugely popular — these models have been downloaded over nine hundred million times. And our latest Gemma 4 models have been downloaded over 300 million times since launching in April.

Our agentic development platform Antigravity allows anyone to build in the agent-first era. It has more than 2.4 million weekly active users. Antigravity is a powerful tool for users and enterprises, and it’s completely accelerated how we build internally. As just one example, a team in Chrome is now on track to accelerate delivery by eight times, compressing a two-year timeline into three months through model-driven refactoring.

Search

Moving on to our global product footprint, which brings AI to more people than any other company.

Starting with Search. AI continues to drive an expansionary moment, with new experiences resonating with users and driving growth in queries. As a big football fan, I was particularly excited to see Search usage hit an all-time high during the World Cup this year. This really highlights how much people turn to Google in moments that matter.

We continue to make Search more helpful and intuitive. We recently brought together AI Overviews and AI Mode into one seamless Search experience that combines our frontier capabilities with the best of the web. And we’re continuing to incorporate more frontier capabilities into Search with agents, personal intelligence, and notebooks.

Our AI-powered features are driving increased Search usage. Since expanding AI Mode globally last October, we’ve surpassed 1 billion monthly active users. And just like AI Overviews, AI Mode is driving an incremental increase in Search queries overall, and we’re now sending billions of clicks to websites every week through AI features in Search.

As we serve more of these queries, we’ve continued to drive efficiencies. Thanks to our engineering and hardware optimizations, this quarter we reduced the cost of AI Mode responses to its lowest level since launch, even as we’ve brought more advanced AI capabilities.

Gemini app

Next, the Gemini app, which now has 950 million monthly active users, with daily active users tripling in the last year. Users love new agentic features like Daily Brief and our personalized agent, Gemini Spark, which is now available in the U.S. and internationally. We’ve been shipping helpful new features like this at an incredible clip.

YouTube

On to YouTube. Month after month, when a big event happens in the world, the world turns to YouTube. It’s been incredible to see that over 1.7 billion unique viewers globally watched World Cup-related videos on YouTube during the FIFA World Cup 2026TM.

We are also bringing the power of conversational AI directly into the YouTube experience. Ask YouTube uses our Gemini models to let people ask complex questions about individual videos, get quick takeaways, and jump straight to moments in those videos. The early engagement is encouraging. More than 140 million users engaged with Ask YouTube on the watch page in June 2026. And we're bringing that Ask experience to the wider search experience on YouTube.

Cloud

Next, Cloud. Our continued momentum is driven by our integrated AI portfolio consisting of chips, models, data, security, and agent platforms — all designed to work together. Gemini continues to be a key driver of growth, and is deeply integrated across all of our Cloud products including Gemini Enterprise, data analytics, cybersecurity, and Google Workspace.

We are seeing strong, diversified demand across products, customers, geographies, and industries. Our product differentiation is driving expansion in three ways:

  • We’re winning new customers, more than doubling our acquisition velocity year-over-year.
  • We’re deepening our relationships with existing customers, who are expanding their usage and exceeding their commitments by more than 50% — also an acceleration over last quarter.
  • We’re driving growth with partners, with transactions on Google Cloud Marketplace growing over seven times year-over-year.

One of the strongest parts of our growth comes from the rapid adoption of our Gemini Enterprise platform. It is differentiated with easy-to-use tools to build agents and automate processes, connectivity to enterprise systems, cost management, and governance tools. In Q2, Agent Development Kit, our framework for building and deploying enterprise AI agents, reached nearly 70 million total downloads.

As I said, nearly 90% of the Fortune 100 are using Gemini Enterprise. We have customers like PepsiCo for AI and analytics solutions, Intel to streamline core processes, HSBC for wealth management, Bell Canada for customer engagement, Macy’s for commerce experiences, and SIGNAL IDUNA for knowledge management.

More broadly, Gemini is transforming how millions of businesses use AI to build custom agents, automate processes, improve cybersecurity, manage customer relationships, streamline data analytics, and more.

All of this momentum is driving growth in our paid token usage. Nearly 500 Cloud customers have each processed more than 1 trillion tokens in the last year, and usage is so much deeper than that. Over the last 12 months, more than 2,000 enterprises consumed over 100 billion tokens.

We are also seeing strong interest in our AI-powered security platform, which is differentiated because it integrates threat intelligence, cyber-response prioritization with Wiz, AI-automated scanning, code remediation, and monitoring. Today:

  • 90% of the Fortune 100 are Google Cloud Security users;
  • Nearly 90% of Wiz customers are using AI-powered security features;
  • And we have seen a more than 45% quarter-over-quarter increase in the number of AI workloads scanned and protected by our security platform.

Our security tools are being used to protect critical infrastructure, including financial services organizations such as Morgan Stanley; telecommunication providers such as Tell-us; healthcare organizations such as Texas Children's; software companies such as Atlassian; and several government agencies. And with our new Google AI Threat Defense, we’re really excited to bring our new cyber model and CodeMender to help our customers defend against AI threats.

All of this is powered by our leading AI infrastructure. We offer the industry’s broadest range of accelerators from Google and NVIDIA, including the new NVIDIA Vera Rubin platform, and TPU 8t and 8i, which deliver strong price performance. On top of this, let me mention three unique offerings:

  • Our Virgo Network, which is designed to meet the needs of modern, large scale AI workloads. It allows customers to connect a million AI accelerators across multiple data center sites into a unified supercomputer.
  • Our software stack has native support for JAX, PyTorch, vLLM, and SGLang, enabling workload portability across GPUs and TPUs.
  • Our new agent-optimized Axion CPUprovides 30% better performance per dollar compared to peer offerings.

We are seeing strong growth in demand for our AI infrastructure offerings from leading labs such as Ineffable Intelligence; next-generation AI builders including Kakao; financial services like Deutsche Börse Group; pharmaceutical companies such as Pfizer and Roche; and robotics and spatial intelligence companies such as World Labs.

Other Bets

We are continuing our strategy of investing in promising companies and internal efforts that bring pioneering AI research into the real world. We’ve seen exciting progress from our Other Bets:

  • Waymo introduced its newest vehicle, Ojai, to public riders. This is the first vehicle powered by the sixth-generation Waymo Driver and will welcome more riders in the coming months.
  • Wing has safely completed more than 1 million home deliveries, and we continue to grow our presence through partnerships with Walmart, DoorDash, and Papa Johns.
  • In health and drug discovery, Isomorphic Labs raised over $2 billion to power its AI drug design engine, scale globally, and advance its drug candidate pipeline.

It’s truly been an extraordinary first half of 2026, with much more to come. Thanks to all of our employees and partners worldwide for their incredible work this quarter.

entries.title: Here’s how to ask Gemini Live for help with anything you see.
entries.summary: Have you ever struggled to describe something you’re looking at? Whether it’s a complex manual, a blinking error code, or a unique object, sometimes you just need an exp…
entries.content:
Have you ever struggled to describe something you’re looking at? Whether it’s a complex manual, a blinking error code, or a unique object, sometimes you just need an expert to see what you see. With the Gemini app, you can skip the long descriptions and capture exactly what’s in front of you.

Simply tap the Live icon and click the camera to start Gemini Live, and point your lens at whatever you need help with. Ask Gemini for step-by-step repair instructions or tips for organizing messy drawers. Or share your screen to get assistance navigating a confusing app.

You can even get creative, using Gemini to brainstorm decor ideas or give you personalized advice as you shop. Get real-time help for anything you can think of, using the real world as your starting point.

entries.title: Switch to Android easily — and bring your data with you.
entries.summary: A new migration experience built directly into Android 17 that lets you transfer more data wirelessly from an iPhone.
entries.content:
Switching to Android just got even easier. With the launch of Samsung’s new Z Series at today’s Galaxy Unpacked, we introduced a new native migration experience built directly into Android 17 that lets you transfer even more data types from an iPhone wirelessly — without downloading a separate app.

With the upgraded method, you can easily bring over what matters most. This includes your photos, videos, contacts, messages, and calendar — plus, newly supported data, such as your Google Account, passwords, Wi-Fi credentials, and even your eSIM.

This upgrade has already started rolling out to select Pixel devices. It will also be available on the new Samsung Galaxy Z Flip8 and Z Fold8 series devices and come to more Android devices soon.

Visit android.com/switch for more switching to Android tips.

entries.title: 3 Google updates from Galaxy Unpacked 2026
entries.summary: Gentle Monster glasses, Warby Parker glasses, a prompt asking for the history behind a pictured building, and a prompt asking to book a table at a pictured restaurant
entries.content:
Today at Galaxy Unpacked in London, we celebrated the launch of the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra, Fold8, and Flip8. With these devices, we are making it easier than ever to get started on Android while also rolling out the first capabilities of Gemini Intelligence 1 — our vision for technology that works proactively for you. Whether you choose to unfold your phone to maximize your productivity on a bigger screen or keep it folded so you can focus on your day, Gemini Intelligence is there to help with the heavy lifting.

Here is a look at three updates coming to the newest devices.

1. Get next-generation task automation with Gemini Intelligence.

Gemini Intelligence can help you offload digital chores so you can spend less time behind a screen. Back in February, we introduced a beta for task automation, helping you delegate simple everyday tasks to Gemini, such as ordering a rideshare or getting your favorite takeout meal for delivery.

Now task automation is expanding support from a handful of apps to more than 40 popular apps. 2 This means Gemini can now help you handle more of your daily "life admin" — from shopping and making dinner reservations to booking travel experiences and buying event tickets. On the Galaxy Z Flip8, you can even access this assistance directly from the Flex Window by long pressing the power button.

Plus, Gemini can now use advanced reasoning to understand what is on your screen and parse complex images as prompts. This gives you a powerful jumpstart — because sometimes, the easiest way to explain what you need is to show Gemini a shopping list or inspiration photo.

While Gemini works to complete the task, you can track the step-by-step progress on your screen, multitask in other apps, or close your phone and let Gemini run in the background. If you need to make a quick adjustment or add a personal touch, you can step in to handle a specific detail and then hand control back to Gemini to complete the rest. Gemini is designed to notify you when the task is ready for your review and final confirmation.

2. Raise the bar on your projects with Gemini Notebook.

To help you manage deep projects and synthesize complex information, we are bringing the power of Gemini Notebook (formerly NotebookLM) directly to your device. The app comes preinstalled on Samsung's new foldables. 3

Gemini Notebook is your thinking and research partner powered by our advanced multimodal models. On the Galaxy Z Fold8 series' expansive display, you can drag and drop photos, documents, whiteboard images, voice recordings, and more directly into a side-by-side workspace.

You can use the chat function in Gemini Notebook to ask questions about your sources or go one step further and create custom, high-quality resources — including slide decks, videos, flash cards, quizzes, or even a podcast — tailored to how you learn best.

To help you tackle your most ambitious projects, we are including a six-month trial of Google AI Pro on every Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra, Fold8, and Flip8, which gives you more access to powerful features, such as Audio Overviews, Video Overviews, Slides, and more.

3. Take Gemini on the go with watches and glasses.

Our collaboration with Samsung extends beyond smartphones. On the Galaxy Watch 9, you have more convenient access to Gemini on the go. Simply raise your wrist and Gemini will activate so you can ask it questions, no wake word required.

We’re also bringing Gemini to glasses. At Google I/O 2026, we announced intelligent eyewear — created in partnership with Samsung and launching this fall — and shared two designs from our partners, eyewear brands Gentle Monster and Warby Parker. Today we revealed two more frames and shared more about how Gemini’s helpfulness will come to life on these new devices. For example if you’re wearing a compatible Wear OS watch, you’ll be able to control your glasses with just a hand gesture.

Whether you are looking to simplify your daily routine or boost your productivity, today's announcements are just the beginning of what we have in store. We will roll out more intelligent features as they are ready, so you’re not left waiting.

entries.title: Drive with Halo on Waze
entries.summary: Image of The Master Chief and Cortana
entries.content:
Building on a partnership that began in 2021, Waze is once again teaming up with Halo to celebrate the launch of Halo: Campaign Evolved, with a refreshed Halo experience. This time, we’re bringing back the legendary Master Chief voice navigation, and introducing his AI companion, Cortana, to drivers for the very first time.

This updated experience brings the iconic Master Chief back to your dashboard. His lines are refreshed but his legendary attitude remains exactly the same, giving you tactical updates like, “This is Spartan 117. Vehicle Delivery Confirmed. Let’s finish this mission.”

Cortana brings heart, wry humor, and a humanizing touch to the drive, no small feat for an AI hologram. She serves as the perfect guide for drivers stepping into the Master Chief’s massive shoes, offering seamless assistance with navigation lines like: “Red light camera reported ahead. Security protocols appear to be operational.”

To further bring you into the new Halo: Campaign Evolved experience, you can choose between Master Chief’s “Stoic” or Cortana’s “Vital” Moods on the Waze map, and swap your standard vehicle for the iconic “Warthog” or “Ghost” to start your mission.

This new driving experience is available globally in English and Spanish. Simply download the latest version of Waze and look for the Halo banner in the menu, or activate it now.

entries.title: 13 Google tips for a fun, productive summer off from college
entries.summary: Illustration of a woman in front of a computer, a phone searching an image of a plant, a suitcase, and a video feed
entries.content:
It's summer in the Northern Hemisphere. That means it’s prime time for beach trips, backyard barbecues, and afternoons at the pool. And if you’re a college student, it’s also the perfect season for getting ahead of your to-do list.

Need to find a fall internship, study for admissions exams, master a new skill, or prep for the school year ahead? Here are 13 ways Google’s AI tools can help you get things done with less stress — so you can spend more time enjoying your summer.

Apply for a fall internship.

1. Not sure where to start your internship search? Brainstorm options with Career Dreamer, an AI-powered tool that helps you explore possible job opportunities. Input your experiences, skills, and interests, and find potential career paths tailored to your background.

2. Build a dashboard to track internship opportunities — and application deadlines — using Canvas in AI Mode. Simply open AI Mode in Search and turn on the Canvas tool to open a dedicated workspace. Then try a conversational prompt, like: “Help me build a dashboard to track fall internships in NYC focused on marketing and communications in fashion. Include details on deadlines and application requirements.”

3. Workshop your resume and cover letter with Gemini Notebook, an AI-powered research partner. Create a notebook for your internship search. Then upload all your career clutter — old projects, positive feedback, resumes, and more. Gemini Notebook can help you fine-tune your application materials, identify key strengths, and turn your background into a coherent narrative to pitch to employers.

4. Prep for upcoming interviews by rehearsing with Gemini Live. You can use this feature to have a natural, free-flowing conversation out loud — just like you would in an interview. Practice answering common interview questions, and ask Gemini for real-time feedback on your delivery, pacing, and tone. It’s like having an interview coach in your pocket.

Try new things.

5. Explore a hobby, pick up a creative project, or master a new skill with YouTube Learning. YouTube’s educational library is packed with top-tier instructional content. And on select videos, you’ll see an “Ask” button that lets you dive even deeper. Tap it to find out what a video covers, generate a summary of its key points, or even create a brief quiz on its contents — all without leaving the video.

6. When you need help picking up a new skill — like crafting the perfect cup of matcha — consider Search Live your very own expert adviser. Just open the Google app (Android and iOS) and tap the Live icon under the search bar. Then point your camera at the equipment in your matcha set and ask what each tool is used for. You can also get quick advice on which ingredients you can substitute if you’re looking for low-sugar or dairy-free alternatives for a matcha latte.

7. Get instant feedback on your sports form with Gemini video analysis. Let’s say you’ve taken up pickleball over the summer. Record a quick clip of yourself hitting a few shots, and ask Gemini how you can improve. It will break down your paddle angle, stance, and follow-through, helping you understand what to adjust.

8. Use Lens in the Google app to dive deeper into your interests, whether you’re expanding your plant collection or exploring local history. Maybe you see a cool plant, but you’re not sure what it’s called or how to take care of it. Snap a photo with Lens, and add “how much sunlight does this need” to learn more. You can do the same with unique landmarks in your area. Take a picture with Lens and ask “what inspired this design?”

9. Maintain healthy digital habits with Android’s built-in screen time tools. When you’re in the zone of learning something new, it’s easy to lose track of time and stare at your screen for hours. So set take a break reminders or a bedtime schedule to keep your days balanced. That way, you can stay refreshed and ready for your summer internship, vacation, or whatever else the season has in store.

Study smarter.

10. Applying to grad school? Use study notebooks in Gemini to prep for standardized tests, like the GRE (available in Gemini later this summer). After assessing your knowledge gaps with a diagnostic quiz, Gemini will keep you on track with bite-sized lessons and progress checks.

11. AI Mode in Search can help you create custom study guides for summer courses, using the Canvas tool. Just select Canvas from the plus (+) menu and ask AI Mode something like “make a study guide for Impressionism, include key periods, and highlight the main artworks.” You’ll get a comprehensive study guide, complete with relevant links and images. Then, you can ask follow-up questions, upload your notes to add more context, and either share a link to the guide with your classmates or export it to Google Docs.

12. Turn your notes into a podcast with Gemini Notebook. Let’s say you’re planning to apply to law school this fall. Upload your LSAT study materials into a notebook. Then use the Audio Overview feature to create a podcast between two AI hosts discussing your notes. You can listen to your custom podcast while riding the subway or walking to your internship. And you can even interrupt the hosts with questions if you don’t understand something.

13. Organize last semester’s notes by creating a dedicated Gemini notebook for each class you took. Upload everything: whiteboard photos, lecture videos, problem sets, class notes — you name it. Gemini can transform your files into whatever format helps you learn best, like a study guide, practice quiz, infographic, or interactive visualization. And when you want to review your notes, try explaining the concept out loud using Gemini Live. It’ll spot the gaps in your knowledge, asking follow-up questions to make sure you still understand the material.
